104: Let us remain calm; at least this parliament stands for dignity and pride
106: and for our Arab identity. I suggest adding a special point on this issue to the parliamentary agenda. Many deputies are upset about these visits, this open normalization, and the boasting about it, which has greatly disturbed us.
108: So, sir president, if you allow us to include this issue now, and
110: allocate at least half an hour for it since the minister has not arrived yet
112: instead of leaving it to the end of the session, why don't we add it now? Let two deputies from the same bloc discuss this topic, denounce it, and make a position of the parliament, the deputies, the people, and sovereignty that we reject normalization in all its forms, whether public or secret, and reject the silence and collusion of Tunisian authorities with this normalization. Unfortunately, since this minister assumed this position, we have witnessed normalization after normalization. Thank you, sir.
114: The president, the first deputy speaker of the Assembly of the Representatives of the People.
116: This is a request to amend the agenda. Allow me to leave.
118: I will review the legal text and report scientifically; the agenda can be amended
120: at the start of the session once quorum is reached, and today’s session is not
122: independent but rather a continuation, as many colleagues mentioned. Does the internal law allow us to add this point or not? Allow me to check the text; we still have time since the session has not started working yet. Thank you. Ms. Lilia Younes Al-Kassebi, please go ahead.
124: Thank you, Mr. President. I want to remind the Council Bureau and its presidency that we have the Constitutional Court law which we have not yet voted on, and it is our duty and in the country's interest to schedule the vote for its four members. We have spent more than three years without agreement, and we are at the end of our parliamentary term. This is unreasonable and dangerous for the country and for democratic gains. It is dangerous to head towards the 2019 legislative elections without having installed the Constitutional Court.
126: This is a danger to the country and its gains. The second issue concerns the constitutional bodies whose members we have not continued electing, such as the Independent Authority for Audiovisual Communication, the Good Governance Authority, the Sustainable Development Authority, and the next generations’ rights law, which we have not yet voted on. Each time this law is scheduled
128: on the agenda of the general session, it is not voted on.
130: Mr. President, I want to remind my colleagues, your Excellency, and the Council Bureau of the trust we hold, which is our duty and the duty of the Tunisians. Thank you, first deputy speaker of the Assembly of the Representatives of the People.
132: Thank you, and I agree with you on this duty, but what mechanism does the Council presidency or bureau have? It only can present this to the deputies and offer it to them, and it seems they have not reached a consensus on it. I will inform the Council Bureau, which will convene at 2:30 p.m. today, and they will inform us
134: of their decision. Thank you. Please allow us to adjourn the session for fifteen minutes. Professor Ziad, please.
136: Go ahead, then Mr. Haikel, Mr. Ziad Al-Akhdar.
140: Mr. President, when a woman arrives in Tunisia under suspicious circumstances saying "Long live Israel" on Tunisian soil where the Zionist entity has spilled the blood of Tunisians and Palestinians in Ennaceur Beach, this is a serious matter. The internal regulations and constitutional governance mean nothing when confronting this phenomenon. Today it is very strange in this parliament that a deputy requests a point of order to talk about this issue while the rest behave as if they are not present.
144: Please, today we are facing a growing influence of the Zionist entity in
146: our country and the Arab world, and we must take responsibility, holding others accountable. We should not allow a strange visit to turn Tunisian land into a spying outpost and allow people seeking to visit the house where the great fighter Abu Jihad was assassinated. This is shameful and requires this parliament to assume its responsibility and question this government and the Minister of the Interior
152: Thank you, first deputy speaker of the Assembly of the Representatives of the People.
160: Not a day passes without this government confirming to us that it is reverting to the spheres of dictatorship, violence, and violations of economic, social, public, and personal rights. Mr. President, the city of Regueb has been subjected to militarization and severe security pressure. Their only fault was that the people of Regueb in all their social, political components, and civil society activists all held a peaceful civic protest demanding the life and beach of Regueb be free from pollution, which we discussed yesterday with the Minister of Local Affairs. The government's response was forceful and violent, using tear gas against women, children, and youth in neighborhoods and homes without respect for human dignity, the constitution, or rights.
162: I want to state here, on behalf of the Popular Front, that we condemn such behavior and will always support every peaceful protest. The minister knows the situation: these people did not demand employment or any personal demands; they only demanded a dignified life on the land and protection for the sea, air, and land of Regueb. The response was reckless and unprecedented in terms of security measures. This is what I told the Minister of Interior during my phone call, and this will only escalate the people's anger, as if we are pouring oil on the fire. Personally, I only support these people in their demands and defending their dignity and cleanliness. This is a message to the Minister of Interior, the Prime Minister, and the regional authorities, and I hope this message is delivered before the situation spirals out of control. Thank you. First deputy speaker of the Assembly of the Representatives of the People. Thank you to my colleagues for their interventions, and we welcome the attendance of His Excellency.
